elements.sent-2411-001: The correlation between the mean transverse momentum, $[p_{\mathrm{T ]$, and the squared anisotropic flow, $v^{2 _{n}$, on an event-by-event basis has been suggested to be influenced by the initial conditions in heavy-ion collisions.}}}
elements.sent-2411-002: We present measurements of the variances and covariance of $[p_{\mathrm{T ]$ and $v^{2 _{n}$, along with their dimensionless ratio, for Au+Au collisions at various beam energies: \roots $=$ 14.6, 19.6, 27, 54.4, and 200~GeV.}}}
elements.sent-2411-003: Our measurements reveal a distinct energy-dependent behavior in the variances and covariance.
elements.sent-2411-004: In addition, the dimensionless ratio displays a similar behavior across different beam energies.
elements.sent-2411-005: We compare our measurements with hydrodynamic models and similar measurements from Pb+Pb collisions at the Large Hadron Collider (LHC).
elements.sent-2411-006: These findings provide valuable insights into the beam energy dependence of the specific shear viscosity ($\eta/s$) and initial-state effects, allowing for differentiating between different initial-state models.
elements.sent-2411-007: Extensive experimental measurements of heavy-ion collisions at the Relativistic Heavy Ion Collider (RHIC)~\cite{STAR:2005gfr, PHENIX:2004vcz, BRAHMS:2004adc, PHOBOS:2004zne and the Large Hadron Collider~\cite{Muller:2012zq demonstrate the formation of the Quark-Gluon Plasma (QGP), as predicted by Quantum Chromodynamics (QCD).}}
elements.sent-2411-008: Many of these investigations seek to characterize the QGP's transport properties (e.i., the specific shear viscosity $\eta / s$)~\cite{Shuryak:1978ij, Shuryak:1980tp .}
elements.sent-2411-009: The anisotropic flow measurements, described by the azimuthal anisotropy of the particles emitted relative to collision symmetry planes, are important in many studies~\cite{Shuryak:2003xe, Luzum:2008cw, Bozek:2009dw, Danielewicz:1998vz, Heinz:2001xi, Hirano:2005xf .}
elements.sent-2411-010: Specifically, anisotropic flow measurements reflect the viscous hydrodynamic response to the initial spatial distribution formed in the collision at early stages~\cite{Huovinen:2001cy, Hirano:2002ds, Romatschke:2007mq, Luzum:2011mm, Song:2010mg, Qian:2016fpi, Schenke:2011tv, Magdy:2020gxf, Magdy:2022ize .}
elements.sent-2411-011: The anisotropic flow can be characterized by the Fourier expansion~\cite{Voloshin:1994mz,Poskanzer:1998yz of the particle azimuthal angle ($\phi$) distributions, \begin{eqnarray}\label{eq:1-1} \frac{dN}{d\phi} \propto 1+ 2 \sum^{\infty}_{n=1}v_{n} \cos\left[ n (\phi - \Psi_{n}) \right] ,\end{eqnarray} where $\Psi_{n}$ is the n$^{th}$-order flow symmetry plane.}
elements.sent-2411-012: The n$^{th}$ complex anisotropic flow vector with \vn magnitude and $\Psi_{n}$ direction is defined as $V_{n = \vn~e^{i n \Psi_{n }$.}}
elements.sent-2411-013: The first three flow coefficients are commonly termed the directed flow (\first), elliptic flow (\second), and triangular flow (\third).
elements.sent-2411-014: Anisotropic flow measurements of the n$^{th}$-order flow harmonics~\cite{PHOBOS:2006dbo, STAR:2002hbo, STAR:2003xyj, PHENIX:2018wex, ALICE:2011ab, ATLAS:2018ezv, CMS:2012tqw, CMS:2012zex, Magdy:2024ooh, Magdy:2019ojv, Adam:2019woz, Magdy:2018itt, Magdy:2023owx, Adamczyk:2017ird, Magdy:2017kji , the correlation between different flow harmonics~\cite{STAR:2018fpo, ALICE:2016kpq, Aad:2015lwa, Qiu:2011iv, Adare:2011tg, Aad:2014fla, Aad:2015lwa , and flow fluctuations~\cite{Magdy:2018itt, Alver:2008zza, Alver:2010rt, Ollitrault:2009ie have led to a deeper understanding of the heavy-ion collision's initial conditions~\cite{Retinskaya:2013gca, Moreland:2014oya and the properties of the matter created in heavy-ion collisions~\cite{Teaney:2003kp, Song:2011qa .}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-015: Extensive comparisons between data and models across various collision energies and systems have indicated a low $\eta/s$ for the QGP, yet significant uncertainties persist~\cite{Shuryak:2003xe, Romatschke:2007mq, Luzum:2008cw, Bozek:2009dw, Song:2010mg, Shen:2011eg .}
elements.sent-2411-016: These uncertainties primarily arise from the difficulty in constraining the initial conditions of the collision.
elements.sent-2411-017: As a result, current theoretical and experimental investigations have sought to identify observables that could offer additional insights and tighter constraints on the initial state of heavy-ion collisions.
elements.sent-2411-018: One recent proposal is to examine the correlation between the mean transverse momentum, $[p_{\mathrm{T ]$, and the squared anisotropic flow, $v^{2 _{n}$, on an event-by-event basis~\cite{Giacalone:2020byk, Bozek:2016yoj, Schenke:2020uqq, Giacalone:2020dln, Lim:2021auv, ATLAS:2021kty .}}}}
elements.sent-2411-019: These correlations are quantified using the Pearson correlation coefficient; \begin{eqnarray}\rho(v^{2}_{n},[p_{T}]) = \frac{{\rm Cov}(v_{n}^{2},[p_T])}{\sqrt{{\rm Var}(v_{n}^{2})} \sqrt{{\rm Var}([p_T])}}\label{eq:1},\end{eqnarray} {\color{black where, $\mathrm{Cov(X,Y)}$ and $\mathrm{Var(X)}$ represent the covariance of parameters $X$ and $Y$ and the variance of parameter $X$ respectively.}}
elements.sent-2411-020: The correlations between $[p_T]$ and the $v^{2 _{n}$ coefficients offer valuable insights into two key aspects: (i) the relationship between the size and eccentricities in the initial state, and (ii) the correlation between the strength of the hydrodynamic response and the flow coefficients.}
elements.sent-2411-021: It is noteworthy that $v^{2 _{n}$ ($\mathrm{Var(v^{2 _{n )}$) is primarily influenced by eccentricities, while $[p_T]$($\mathrm{Var([p_T])}$) is driven by the transverse size of the collision zone.}}}
elements.sent-2411-022: Specifically, events with similar energy density but smaller transverse size tend to exhibit more radial expansion, leading to larger values of $[p_T]$($\mathrm{Var([p_T])}$)~\cite{Bozek:2012fw .}
elements.sent-2411-023: Furthermore, several studies~\cite{Giacalone:2019pca, Giacalone:2020awm, Magdy:2022cvt, ATLAS:2021kty, STAR:2024eky have highlighted the sensitivity of the ${\rho(v^{2 _{n ,[p_{T ])}$ correlation to the initial-state nuclear deformation and the nuclear geometry of the colliding nuclei.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-024: In this letter, we use the multiparticle cumulant method to study the $\rho(v^{2 _{2 ,[p_{T ])$ and its components (${{Var (v_{n ^{2 ) , {Var ([p_T])$ and ${Cov (v_{n ^{2 ,[p_T])$) dependence on beam energy.}}}}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-025: These measurements are expected to provide a unique data set that is comprehensive and derived from a consistent analysis across the different beam energies presented.
elements.sent-2411-026: The Au+Au data reported in this analysis were taken using a minimum-bias trigger, by the STAR detector~\cite{Beddo:1992pv at \roots = 14.6, 19.6, 27.0, 54.4, and 200~GeV collected in the years 2019, 2019, 2018, 2017, and 2011, respectively.}
elements.sent-2411-027: The collision vertices were reconstructed using tracks measured with charged-particle trajectories detected in the STAR Time Projection Chamber (TPC) in a 0.5 T magnetic field pointing along the beam direction (z-axis)~\cite{Anderson:2003ur .}
elements.sent-2411-028: Events were selected to have a vertex position about the nominal center of the TPC (in the beam direction) of $\pm$ 30~cm for Au+Au at $\roots = 200$~GeV, $\pm$ 40 cm at $\roots$ = 54.4 and 27~GeV, and $\pm$ 70 cm at $\roots$ = 19.6 and 14.6~GeV and to be within a radius of $r<2$~cm with respect to the beam axis.
elements.sent-2411-029: This reference multiplicity is typically calculated from the raw multiplicity of primary charged particles reconstructed in the TPC across the entire azimuthal range and within $|\eta|$ $<$ 0.5.
elements.sent-2411-030: The selection of $|\eta|$ $<$ 0.5 is deliberate as it ensures a nearly uniform distribution of $\eta$ in this range of the detector, and the tracks exhibit higher quality compared to those near the TPC edge.
elements.sent-2411-031: Centrality classes are established by fitting the reference multiplicity distribution to that derived from Monte Carlo Glauber simulations~\cite{Adamczyk:2012ku, Abelev:2009bw .}
elements.sent-2411-032: The $\rho(v^{2 _{n ,[p_{T ])$ correlator is derived from covariance and variance (cf.}}}
elements.sent-2411-033: Eq.~\ref{eq:1 ) which involve both two- and multi-particle correlations.}
elements.sent-2411-034: The two- and multi-particle correlations could be influenced by nonflow effects due to resonance decays, Bose-Einstein correlations, and the fragments of individual jets~\cite{Jia:2013tja .}
elements.sent-2411-035: Since nonflow contributions mainly involve particles emitted within a localized region in pseudorapidity, $\mathrm{\eta}$, they can be suppressed via sub-event cumulant methods~\cite{Jia:2017hbm, Huo:2017nms, Zhang:2018lls, Magdy:2020bhd, Zhang:2021phk .}
elements.sent-2411-036: In the sub-event cumulant methods, the correlated particles are taken from two or more sub-events that are separated in $\mathrm{\eta}$.
elements.sent-2411-037: The efficiency of these methods to reduce nonflow effects has been quantified for many different two- and multi-particle correlators~\cite{Jia:2017hbm, Huo:2017nms, Magdy:2020bhd, Zhang:2021phk, Magdy:2022ize, Magdy:2022jai .}
elements.sent-2411-038: The traditional and two-subevent cumulant methods, which incorporate weights to account for acceptance and efficiency~\cite{Jia:2017hbm , are employed in this study to construct the $v_{n ^{2}$ variance.}}
elements.sent-2411-039: Specifically, the $\eta$ range of $0.35<|\eta|<1.0$ is selected to measure the two-particle $v_{n}\{2\}$ and the four-particle $v_{2}\{4\}$ using the two-subevent and traditional cumulant methods, respectively.
elements.sent-2411-040: Two subevents, A and C are defined, where all tracks in sub-event A are required to be within $-1.0< \eta_{A <-0.35$ and all tracks in sub-event C are required to be within $0.35<\eta_{C <1.0$.}}
elements.sent-2411-041: The $v_{n ^{2}$ dynamical variance is given as~\cite{Bozek:2021zim : \begin{eqnarray}\label{eq:2-1} {\rm Var}(v_{2}^{2})_{dyn} = v_{2}\{2\}^{4} - v_{2}\{4\}^{4} \nonumber, = C^{2}_{2}\{2\} - C_{2}\{4\} \nonumber, {\rm Var}(v_{3}^{2})_{dyn} = v_{3}\{2\}^{4} = C^{2}_{3}\{2\}.\end{eqnarray}.}}
elements.sent-2411-042: The two-particle flow harmonics $v_{n}\{2\}$ can be given as: \begin{eqnarray}\label{eq:2-2} C_{n}\{2\} = \langle \langle 2_n\rangle\rangle|_{AC} = \langle \langle e^{i~ n (\varphi^{A}_{1} - \varphi^{C}_{2} )} \rangle \rangle, \nonumber v_{n}\{2\} = \sqrt{C_{n}\{2\}}\end{eqnarray} where $\varphi_{A(C)}$ is the azimuthal angle of particles in the region $A$($C$) and {\color{black $\langle \langle 2_n\rangle\rangle$ is the two particle correlations of order $n$}.}
elements.sent-2411-043:
In addition, the four-particle flow harmonic $v_{2}\{4\}$ can be given as:  egin{eqnarray}\label{eq:2-3} C_{2}\{4\} = \langle \langle 4_2angleangle - 2 \langle \langle 2_2angleangle^{2}, 
onumber = \langle \langle e^{i~ 2 (\varphi_{1} + \varphi_{2} - \varphi_{3} - \varphi_{4})} angleangle 
onumber - ~2~ \langle \langle e^{i~ 2 (\varphi_{1} - \varphi_{2} )} angleangle 
onumber v_{2}\{4\} = \sqrt[4]{-C_{2}\{4\}, }\end{eqnarray} where {\color{black $\langle \langle 4_2angleangle$ is the four particle correlations of order $2$.}}
elements.sent-2411-044: The event mean $p_T$, $[p_T]$, is given as, \begin{eqnarray}\label{eq:2-6} [p_T] = \sum^{M_{B}}_{i=1} p_{T,i} / M_{B},\end{eqnarray} where $M_{B}$ is the event multiplicity in sub-event $B$.
elements.sent-2411-045: The covariance between $v_{2 ^{2}$ and $[p_T]$, ${\rm Cov (v_{2 ^{2 ,[p_T])$, is defined using the three-subevents method~\cite{Aad:2019fgl,Zhang:2021phk as, \begin{eqnarray}\label{eq:2-7} {\rm Cov}(v_{n}^{2},[p_T]) = \left\langle \langle 2_n \rangle|_{AC} \left( [p_T] - \langle [p_T] \rangle \right)_{B} \right\rangle.\end{eqnarray}.}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-046: The resulting $\rho(v^{2 _{2 ,[p_{T ])$ correlator~\cite{Giacalone:2020byk,Lim:2021auv,Bozek:2016yoj,Bozek:2020drh,Schenke:2020uqq,Giacalone:2020dln,ATLAS:2021kty , is obtained via Eqs.~\ref{eq:2-1 , \ref{eq:2-5 and \ref{eq:2-7 : \begin{eqnarray}\label{eq:2-8} \rho(v^{2}_{n},[p_{T}]) = \frac{{\rm Cov}(v_{n}^{2},[p_T])}{\sqrt{{\rm Var}(v_{n}^{2})_{dyn} ~ C_k} }.\end{eqnarray}.}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-047: The presented measurement's systematic uncertainties are evaluated from variations in the analysis cuts for event selection, track selection, and nonflow suppression.
elements.sent-2411-048: The overall systematic uncertainty, assuming independent sources, was taken to be the quadrature sum of the uncertainties resulting from the respective cut variations.
elements.sent-2411-049: Depending on beam energy, they range from 5\% to 9\% from central to peripheral collisions.
elements.sent-2411-050: The $\Delta\eta$ variation is the dominant systematic uncertainty source.
elements.sent-2411-051: The comprehensive beam energy measurements reported in this paper help theoretical models constrain their initial conditions and better estimate how \etas varies with baryon chemical potential and temperature.
elements.sent-2411-052: Hence, it is instructive to compare our measurements to available theoretical predictions.
elements.sent-2411-053: In this work, our measurements at \roots = 200 GeV are compared to two theoretical models: (i) The IP-Glasma+(3+1)D MUSIC+UrQMD model~\cite{Schenke:2019ruo, Schenke:2020uqq , which utilizes an initial state generated from the IP-Glasma framework, followed by the MUSIC viscous hydrodynamic model with \etas of 0.12, and the UrQMD afterburner.}
elements.figure-2411-001: \includegraphics[width=1.0 \linewidth, angle=0]{Fig1.eps} \caption{ Comparison of the centrality dependence of (a) ${\rm Var}(v_{2}^{2})_{dyn}$, (b) $C_{k}$, (c) ${\rm Cov}(v_{2}^{2},[p_T])$, and (d) $\rho(v^{2}_{2},[p_{T}])$, for Au+Au collisions at \roots = 200~GeV. Measurements are compared to theoretical calculations represented as hatched bands~\cite{Schenke:2019ruo} and dashed curve~\cite{Alba:2017hhe}. } \label{Fig:1}
elements.figure-2411-002: \includegraphics[width=1.0 \linewidth,angle=0]{Fig2.eps} \caption{ Comparison of the centrality dependence of (a) ${\rm Var}(v_{3}^{2})_{dyn}$, (b) $C_{k}$, (c) ${\rm Cov}(v_{3}^{2},[p_T])$, and (d) $\rho(v^{3}_{2},[p_{T}])$, for Au+Au collisions at \roots = 200~GeV. Measurements are compared to theoretical calculations represented as hatched bands~\cite{Schenke:2019ruo} and dashed curve~\cite{Alba:2017hhe}. } \label{Fig:2}
elements.sent-2411-054: Figure~\ref{Fig:1 shows the centrality dependence of (a) ${\rm Var (v_{2 ^{2 )_{dyn}$, (b) $C_{k}$, (c) ${\rm Cov (v_{2 ^{2 ,[p_T])$, and (d) $\rho(v^{2 _{2 ,[p_{T ])$ for Au+Au at \roots = 200 GeV.}}}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-055: The shaded bands in panels (a)--(d) and the dashed curve in panel (d) give the viscous hydrodynamic predictions by the IP-Glasma+MUSIC+UrQMD model~\cite{Schenke:2019ruo and the hydrodynamic model with Trento initial conditions~\cite{Alba:2017hhe .}}
elements.sent-2411-056: Although, the hydrodynamic model calculations~\cite{Schenke:2019ruo show good agreement with the ${\rm Var (v_{2 ^{2 )_{dyn}$ and $\sqrt{c_{k }$, it overpredicts the ${\rm Cov (v_{2 ^{2 ,[p_T])$ and $\rho(v^{2 _{2 ,[p_{T ])$ measurements.}}}}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-057: In addition, the two theoretical calculations show a different magnitude for the $\rho(v^{2 _{2 ,[p_{T ])$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-058: Figure~\ref{Fig:2 shows the centrality dependence of the ${\rm Var (v_{3 ^{2 )_{dyn}$ (a), $C_{k}$ (b), ${\rm Cov (v_{3 ^{2 ,[p_T])$ (c), and $\rho(v^{2 _{3 ,[p_{T ])$ (d) for Au+Au at \roots = 200 GeV.}}}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-059: The shaded bands in panels (a)--(d) and the dashed curve in panel (d) show the viscous hydrodynamic calculations of the presented quantities~\cite{Alba:2017hhe, Schenke:2019ruo .}
elements.sent-2411-060: The theoretical calculations~\cite{Schenke:2019ruo show reasonable agreement with the ${\rm Var (v_{3 ^{2 )_{dyn}$, $C_{k}$, and the ${\rm Cov (v_{3 ^{2 ,[p_T])$, however, they over predicts the $\rho(v^{2 _{3 ,[p_{T ])$ measurements in central collisions.}}}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-061: Data-model comparisons of both $\rho(v^{2 _{2 ,[p_{T ])$ and $\rho(v^{2 _{3 ,[p_{T ])$, Figs~\ref{Fig:1 and~\ref{Fig:2 , can be used to differentiate between different theoretical models.}}}}}}}}
elements.figure-2411-003: \includegraphics[width=1.0 \linewidth,angle=0]{Fig3.eps} \caption{ Comparison of the centrality dependence of (a) ${\rm Var}(v_{3}^{2})_{dyn}$, (b) $C_{k}$, (c) ${\rm Cov}(v_{3}^{2},[p_T])$, and (d) $\rho(v^{3}_{2},[p_{T}])$, for Au+Au collisions at \roots = 200, 54.4, 27, 19.6, and 14.6~GeV. The curve represents the LHC measurements~\cite{ALICE:2021gxt}. } \label{Fig:3}
elements.sent-2411-062: Figure~\ref{Fig:3 shows the centrality dependence of ${\rm Var (v_{2 ^{2 )_{dyn}$ (a), $C_{k}$ (b), ${\rm Cov (v_{2 ^{2 ,[p_T])$ (c), and $\rho(v^{2 _{2 ,[p_{T ])$ (d) at five different beam energies.}}}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-063: The measurements in panels (a)--(c) show that the magnitudes of ${\rm Var (v_{2 ^{2 )_{dyn}$, $\sqrt{c_{k }$, and ${\rm Cov (v_{2 ^{2 ,[p_T])$ decreases with beam energy over the full centrality range.}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-064: Such behavior would reflect the beam energy dependence of the final state effect, given that the initial state is not a strong function in beam energy~\cite{Magdy:2021ocp, Magdy:2021cci, STAR:2022gki, STAR:2022vkx .}
elements.sent-2411-065: In contrast, the values for $\rho(v^{2 _{2 ,[p_{T ])$ panel (d) show weak beam energy dependance.}}}
elements.sent-2411-066: The $\rho(v^{2 _{2 ,[p_{T ])$ are compared to published LHC measurements at 5.02 TeV~\cite{ALICE:2021gxt .}}}}
elements.sent-2411-067: The difference between RHIC and LHC measurements could arise from the $[p_{T ]$ difference between the two measurements.}
elements.sent-2411-068: The beam energy measurements and their comparisons to the LHC measurements~\cite{ALICE:2021gxt indicate that the beam energy dependence of ${\rm Var (v_{2 ^{2 )_{dyn}$ and ${\rm Cov (v_{2 ^{2 ,[p_T])$ could potentially provide important constraints for $\eta/s$.}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-069: In contrast, the measurements for $\rho(v^{2 _{2 ,[p_{T ])$ provide complimentary constraints for the initial-state eccentricity and its fluctuations.}}}

The curve represents the LHC measurements~\cite{ALICE:2021gxt}. } \label{Fig:4}

elements.sent-2411-076: In summary, we have presented new $p_{T}$-integrated measurements of the charge-inclusive, ${\rm C_{k }$, ${\rm Var (v_{n ^{2 )_{dyn}$, ${\rm Cov (v_{n ^{2 ,[p_T])$ and $\rho(v_{n ^{2 ,[p_{T ])$ for Au+Au collisions at \roots = 200, 54.4, 27, 19.6 and 14.6~GeV.}}}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-077: Our measurements are compared with similar LHC measurements for Pb+Pb collisions at \roots = 5.02~TeV.
elements.sent-2411-078: The measurements show characteristic patterns for the beam energy dependence of the variances of $[p_{T ]$ and $v_n^2$, and the covariance ${\rm Cov (v_{2 ^{2 ,[p_T])$, consistent with the change of the final state effect with decreasing the beam energy.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-079: By contrast, the correlation coefficient $\rho(v^{2 _{n ,[p_{T ])$ is shown to have weak dependent on beam energy.}}}
elements.sent-2411-080: These measurements suggest that the beam energy dependence of ${\rm C_{k }$, ${\rm Var (v_n^2)_{dyn}$, ${\rm Cov (v_{n ^{2 ,[p_T])$ and $\rho(v^{2 _{n ,[p_{T ])$, could aid in a precision extraction of the temperature and baryon-chemical-potential dependence of $\eta/s$.}}}}}}}}
